mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is Kafka API compatible, designed to be faster and simpler to operate without the need for ZooKeeper or JVM. It is ideal for developers looking for a lightweight alternative to traditional streaming platforms. Key trade-offs include the need for careful resource management and potential vendor lock-in due to its unique architecture.

steps.title: Install the Redpanda CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Redpanda CLI tool, which is necessary for managing Redpanda clusters.

```bash

curl -1sLf 'https://dl.redpanda.com/nzc4ZYQK3WRGd9sy/redpanda/cfg/setup/bash.deb.sh' | sudo -E bash

sudo apt-get install redpanda

```

> This command sets up the repository for Debian/Ubuntu.
> This command installs the Redpanda CLI.

steps.title: Create a Redpanda cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Redpanda cluster on your Kubernetes environment with specified version and node count.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redpanda

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/redpanda.yaml

```

> Create a namespace for Redpanda.
> Deploy Redpanda using the provided YAML file.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the Redpanda cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=~/.kube/config

```

> Ensure your kubeconfig is set to the correct path.

steps.title: Verify Redpanda nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Redpanda nodes to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redpanda

```

> List all pods in the Redpanda namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for Redpanda.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda deployment and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/redpanda.yaml

```

> This command deletes the Redpanda namespace and all resources within it.
> Remove the Redpanda deployment.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your data. This step will upgrade Redpanda to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/redpanda.yaml

kubectl get pods -n redpanda

```

> Drain the nodes before upgrade.
> Reapply the deployment to upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Redpanda pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources allocated to the pod.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in the deployment YAML and redeploy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service configuration and ensure the correct ports are open.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No nodes available for Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Redpanda nodes are not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' to check pod status and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage for Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when the persistent volume claims are not met.

**Fix:**
Ensure that your storage class has enough capacity and adjust the PVCs as needed.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for large message handling and added support for tiered storage.
versionNotes.deprecations: Removed support for legacy configuration files.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.2.x, ensure to migrate your configuration to the new format.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' showing all pods in 'Running' status.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements for Redpanda.
